segments.text: Why are people spending $400 on a perfume? Because of the cap. This brand did not make a product. They engineered an obsession. Most luxury perfumes use boring glass bottles to look premium.
Penhalians did the opposite. They used heavy brass animal heads, a stag, a lion. It looks insane, but design alone
does not create obsession. So, they went much deeper. Each animal has a name, a backstory, and a secret. The stag,
that's Lord George. Old money, tar past,
dangerous charm. You're not buying the cologne. You're choosing what you want to be tonight. And then they also connected them. Lord George has a secret lover. She has her own perfume, her own
story, her own secrets. Now you need both. They turned a product into a story you physically collect. And once you're in, you just can't stop.
